content.full_text:
Kevin L. Reager 
Technical Assistance 
(916) 322-5662 
Manager of Financial Reporting 
Safeco Title Insurance Company 
13640 Roscoe Blvd. 
Panorama City, CA 
91409 
Dear Mr. Reager: 
Administration 
322-.5660 
Executive/legal 
•• 
Enforcement 
322.5901 
322-6441 
September 17, 1984 
RE: 
Our File No. A-84-225 
In response to your letter of August 24, 1984, this is to 
confirm my advice to you that under current California law, 
corporate campaign contributions to statewide officeholders are 
neither prohibited nor limited. 
However, if a corporation makes 
contributions to state or local officeholders, candidates or 
committees totaling $5,000 in a calendar year, the corporation 
will become a "committee" as defined in Government Code Section 
82013(c) and must file campaign disclosure statements. 
You may 
also wish to note that recent legislation will raise the $5,000 
threshold to $10,000 in January 1985, and that Proposition 40 on 
the November 6, 1984 general election ballot will, if approved by 
the voters, prohibit corporate campaign contributions in 
California. 
I hope this information is helpful. 
Please let me know if 
you have any further questions. 
Sincerely, 
(J4A.~~ 'vu~ 
Carla J.

California State Fair Political

1100 K Street 2nd Floor 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Attn: 
Ms. Clara Wordlow 
Dear Ms. Wordlow: 
(213) 873-7788 
Our corporation is considering making a corporate campaign contribution to a 
state office holder. 
I would like to confirm with you that there are no 
current limits on contributions to statewide office holders. 
The only 
requirement being that if the aggregate of all contributions exceeds $5,000 
then form 461 must be filed. 
I would appreciate your verification of my understanding of the situation. 
Sincerely, 
~::::+

sections.question: Our corporation is considering making a corporate campaign contribution to a state office holder. I would like to confirm with you that there are no current limits on contributions to statewide office holders. The only requirement being that if the aggregate of all contributions exceeds $5,000 then form 461 must be filed.
sections.conclusion: Under current California law, corporate campaign contributions to statewide officeholders are neither prohibited nor limited. However, if a corporation makes contributions to state or local officeholders, candidates or committees totaling $5,000 in a calendar year, the corporation will become a "committee" as defined in Government Code Section 82013(c) and must file campaign disclosure statements.
sections.question_synthetic: Are there limits on corporate campaign contributions to statewide officeholders, and what disclosure requirements apply?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Corporate contributions to statewide officeholders are not limited under current law, but corporations must file disclosure statements if total contributions reach $5,000 in a calendar year. The FPPC also notes upcoming legislative changes and a ballot measure that may alter these rules.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both incoming request letter (August 24, 1984) and FPPC response letter (September 17, 1984). File number A-84-225. Response from Carla J. Wordlow, Political Reform Consultant. Clear question and conclusion sections present in the FPPC response.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Our corporation is considering making a corporate campaign contribution to a state office holder. I would like to confirm with you that there are no current limits on contributions to statewide office holders. The only requirement being that if the aggregate of all contributions exceeds $5,000 then form 461 must be filed.

CONCLUSION: Under current California law, corporate campaign contributions to statewide officeholders are neither prohibited nor limited. However, if a corporation makes contributions to state or local officeholders, candidates or committees totaling $5,000 in a calendar year, the corporation will become a "committee" as defined in Government Code Section 82013(c) and must file campaign disclosure statements.

embedding.summary: FPPC responds to Safeco Title Insurance Company's inquiry confirming that corporate campaign contributions to statewide officeholders are not prohibited or limited under current law, but disclosure filing requirements are triggered at $5,000 in annual contributions.
